title: Interpret Shutdown-State AGV Replacement Behavior Through the Hospital
summary: Reference runbook based on a training example describing expected AGV behavior during shutdown-state replacement handling. In the example, an AGV is removed without a tote and another AGV is added; the added AGV goes through the hospital first and then determines where an AGV is missing.
when_to_use: Use this runbook when reviewing or interpreting a shutdown-state scenario in which one AGV was removed without a tote and another AGV was added, and you need to compare observed behavior to the training example for expected routing through the hospital.
not_for: Do not use this as a complete procedure for removing or adding AGVs.
Do not use this to infer broader AGV routing logic beyond the specific shutdown-state example described in the source.
Do not use this for non-shutdown-state AGV behavior interpretation unless separately supported by source evidence.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe the shutdown-state replacement scenario and compare AGV movement to the documented training example.

safety_notes: This is an interpretation/reference runbook, not a control procedure.
The source warns by omission that it does not provide the actual add/remove AGV procedure; only the resulting behavior is described.
Do not generalize the example into unsupported routing or control logic.
access_or_tools_needed: Awareness that the system is in shutdown state
Ability to observe AGV removal and added AGV movement
Access to the training explanation of shutdown-state replacement behavior

access_required: Access to the training explanation or source segment covering shutdown-state replacement behavior
system_preconditions: Scenario includes an AGV removed without a tote
Scenario includes another AGV being added

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing modularized flow between tipper, hospital, and charger areas, including hospital entrance/exit concepts referenced in the shutdown-state example.

steps.title: Confirm the scenario matches the training example
steps.purpose: Ensure the observed case matches the specific shutdown-state replacement example described by the source before applying the interpretation.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the observed scenario is a shutdown-state example in which an AGV was removed without a tote and another AGV was added.
steps.expected_result: The observed case matches the source example closely enough to use this reference.
steps.healthy_condition: System is in shutdown state, the removed AGV had no tote, and a replacement AGV was added.
steps.failure_condition: The observed case does not match the source example, or key conditions are unknown.

actions.label: Match scenario to source example
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the scenario matches the source example: shutdown state, an AGV removed without a tote, and another AGV added.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital area and modular flow context referenced by the training explanation for shutdown-state AGV handling.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the system is not in shutdown state.
Stop if the removed AGV was not removed without a tote.
Stop if there was no added AGV to compare against the example.

source_evidence.text: Confirm that the scenario matches the source example: shutdown state, an AGV removed without a tote, and another AGV added.
Transcript gives a shutdown-state example where removing an AGV without a tote and adding another AGV causes the new AGV to go through the hospital first and then determine where an AGV is missing.

steps.title: Verify the added AGV goes through the hospital first
steps.purpose: Check the primary expected movement described in the training example.
steps.instruction: Observe the added AGV and verify that it goes through the hospital first rather than directly to another location.
steps.expected_result: The added AGV routes through the hospital first.
steps.healthy_condition: The added AGV first moves through the hospital as described in the source example.
steps.failure_condition: The added AGV bypasses the hospital or its path cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Observe hospital-first routing
actions.exact_action: Verify that the added AGV goes through the hospital first rather than directly to another location.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital flow and entrance queue concepts supporting the expectation that the added AGV goes through the hospital first.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the shutdown-state replacement scenario is present but the added AGV does not go through the hospital first as described.

source_evidence.text: Verify that the added AGV goes through the hospital first rather than directly to another location.
Transcript gives a shutdown-state example where removing an AGV without a tote and adding another AGV causes the new AGV to go through the hospital first and then determine where an AGV is missing.

In a shutdown-state example from training video segment 0020, an added AGV first goes through the hospital after another AGV is removed, then determines where it is needed based on a missing AGV condition.

steps.title: Observe how the AGV determines where it is needed
steps.purpose: Confirm the second part of the documented behavior after hospital routing.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the AGV then determines where an AGV is missing, described in the source as going to the shorter side.
steps.expected_result: After going through the hospital, the AGV determines where it is needed based on a missing AGV condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV proceeds from the hospital and then identifies the area where an AGV is missing, consistent with the source description.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV does not appear to determine where an AGV is missing, or observed behavior conflicts with the source example.

actions.label: Observe post-hospital destination logic
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the AGV then determines where an AGV is missing, described in the source as going to the shorter side.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall modular flow context after hospital movement; use this only as visual support for the narrated example.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the AGV behavior after hospital routing conflicts with the documented example.

source_evidence.text: Observe whether the AGV then determines where an AGV is missing, described in the source as going to the shorter side.
Transcript gives a shutdown-state example where removing an AGV without a tote and adding another AGV causes the new AGV to go through the hospital first and then determine where an AGV is missing.

In a shutdown-state example from training video segment 0020, an added AGV first goes through the hospital after another AGV is removed, then determines where it is needed based on a missing AGV condition.

steps.title: Record the behavior as the documented training example
steps.purpose: Capture that the observed behavior matches the source-supported reference example.
steps.instruction: Record that this hospital-first movement is the documented behavior for the shutdown-state replacement example.
steps.expected_result: The observation is documented as matching the training example.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed behavior is recorded specifically as a match to the shutdown-state replacement example from the source.
steps.failure_condition: The behavior cannot be confidently matched to the source example or is recorded too broadly.

actions.label: Document source-aligned interpretation
actions.exact_action: Record that this hospital-first movement is the documented behavior for the shutdown-state replacement example.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if documentation would require broader claims than the source supports.

source_evidence.text: Record that this hospital-first movement is the documented behavior for the shutdown-state replacement example.
This is an interpretation candidate based on a narrated example, not a complete add/remove AGV procedure.
Do not generalize this example into broader routing logic beyond what the source explicitly states.

steps.title: Escalate if observed behavior does not match the example
steps.purpose: Prevent unsupported conclusions when the observed AGV path differs from the documented training example.
steps.instruction: If the added AGV does not follow the hospital-first path described in the source example, stop and escalate.
steps.expected_result: A mismatch is escalated rather than interpreted as normal behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: Any deviation from the documented example is recognized and escalated.
steps.failure_condition: A mismatch is ignored, or unsupported routing conclusions are made.

actions.label: Stop and escalate on mismatch
actions.exact_action: If the added AGV does not follow the hospital-first path described in the source example, stop and escalate.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the shutdown-state replacement scenario is present but the added AGV does not go through the hospital first as described.
Escalate if the scenario appears similar but key conditions cannot be confirmed from observation.

source_evidence.text: If the added AGV does not follow the hospital-first path described in the source example, stop and escalate.
Escalate if the shutdown-state replacement scenario is present but the added AGV does not go through the hospital first as described.

success_criteria: The observed shutdown-state replacement scenario matches the source example conditions.
The added AGV is observed going through the hospital first.
The added AGV is then observed determining where an AGV is missing, consistent with the source example.
The behavior is documented as a source-specific training example without overgeneralization.
healthy_conditions: Added AGV goes through the hospital first.
Added AGV then determines where it is needed based on a missing AGV condition.
failure_conditions: Observed scenario does not match the source example.
Added AGV does not go through the hospital first.
Observed behavior is generalized beyond what the source explicitly states.
The source is treated as a full operational add/remove procedure when it is only a narrated example.
escalation_guidance: Escalate when the shutdown-state replacement scenario is present but the added AGV does not go through the hospital first as described.
Escalate when key scenario conditions cannot be confirmed from observation.
Escalate to SME review if broader routing interpretation is needed beyond the narrated example.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital area and modularized flow referenced by the training explanation.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Transcript gives a shutdown-state example where removing an AGV without a tote and adding another AGV causes the new AGV to go through the hospital first and then determine where an AGV is missing.

missing_details: The source does not provide the actual operational steps for removing an AGV or adding an AGV.
The source does not define the exact system indicators or UI screens used to confirm shutdown state in this example.
The source does not specify formal escalation targets or routing.
The source provides a narrated example rather than a complete diagnostic or recovery workflow.
